evals.prompt: I just finished a feature that adds a new GUC for tuning autovacuum aggressiveness. I have it on a feature branch in dev/. Walk me through getting this on the pgsql-hackers list as a v1 patch.
evals.intent: End-to-end first-time submission: pre-flight, tests/docs, format-patch -v1, cover letter, CommitFest registration.

evals.prompt: My CommitFest entry just got flipped to 'Waiting on Author' — the reviewer left several comments asking for a wider test case and pointed out one of my error messages is wrong style. What's the next step and how do I post the updated patch?
evals.intent: Mid-cycle revision: address feedback, generate v(N+1), reply on existing thread, flip CF status back to Needs Review.

evals.prompt: I want to push a small bug fix upstream — it's a one-line fix in src/backend/utils/adt/numeric.c plus a regress test. Do I really need to do the whole CommitFest dance for something this small, or can I just email it?
evals.intent: Triage: small bug fix still goes via -hackers + CF; design-thread step can be skipped; whether to backpatch is committer's call.
